page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon CPU running at 2.8 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The system is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1.0 GB per core ratio. Cache specifications include 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 33 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark results place the server's single-core and multi-core CPU performance in the average tier, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1409.67 and a multi-core score of 7430.34. Memory bandwidth tests show 412.9 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 48.32 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The instance exhibits weaker performance in data compression and decompression, with single-threaded gzip compression at 23.65 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 25.94 GB/sec falling into the bottom 25% tier. Geekbench multi-core and ray tracing scores also rank in the lower percentile.

Due to its 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the n2-highcpu-8 offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy tasks that do not require a large memory footprint. However, the limited 8.0 GB of RAM represents a significant bottleneck for memory-intensive applications, as reflected in its weak PassMark memory score of 1328.72. This server is best suited for workloads such as software compilation, text processing, static web serving, and lightweight database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on 4 physical cores with hyperthreading, it includes 33 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX-512 vector extensions. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average CPU capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 7430.34 and a PassMark CPU score of 8305.34, though memory-bound performance and data compression tasks fall into the lower percentiles. This configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for CPU-heavy, low-memory workloads. It is well-suited for software compilation, text processing, static web serving, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8 GB of RAM. It offers average compute performance for CPU-bound workloads but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and average CPU benchmark performance.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual server with dedicated Intel Xeon processors, designed for CPU-intensive workloads requiring minimal memory allocation.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor
8.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Supports AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration
Average performance in software compilation and text processing
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for static web serving and lightweight database tasks
